item.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
item.description: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.

item.title: Call for button battery redesign after new study reveals horrific injuries and fatalities
item.description: A new study reveals the horrific injuries suffered after swallowing or inserting button batteries but manufacturers aren't prioritising their redesign.

item.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
item.description: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.

item.title: Reserve Bank restructure closer to reality after Treasurer Jim Chalmers offers olive branch to Angus Taylor
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia is a step closer to its biggest restructure in three decades after the treasurer offers an assurance to his Coalition counterpart.

item.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
item.description: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

item.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

item.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
item.description: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

item.title: Australians tell ABC's Your Say they are struggling more than ever with housing costs
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

item.title: Australia's growing cohort of tenants fear they will rent forever
item.description: Home ownership rates in Australia have been falling for decades, and with rental vacancies at record lows and prices at historic highs, many fear their dream of owning a home may be out of reach. Some experts say rental caps could help.

item.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
item.description: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

item.title: Housing advocates seek federal funding election promise for co-op model
item.description: Advocates admit co-operative housing models are not for everyone but say for the right residents, they offer more security and affordable pricing than traditional rentals.

item.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election
item.description: Young people in Albury say issues that directly affect them and their communities are not being addressed by major parties for the 2025 election campaign.

item.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
item.description: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

item.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
item.description: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

item.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
item.description: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

item.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
item.description: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

item.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
item.description: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

item.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
item.description: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

item.title: Fight against fire ants at tipping point
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rider, Stacey Smith completes a full run at the Topar campdraft in April this year.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Diver describes extreme and hostile claustrophobia inside the Laos cave
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Matildas star Mary Fowler reflects on FA Cup and WSL win with Manchester City
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: Maroons coach explains Reece Walsh selection
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World's largest whale graveyard found in the Indian Ocean
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The $12 Billion World Cup: Inside FIFA’s Biggest Gamble
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: Tony Popovic on playing and coaching in World Cups
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: One goal, many journeys
item.description: The Socceroos squad is like no other that's ever been sent to represent us at a World Cup. See the extraordinary journeys that forged a team to represent modern Australia on the world stage.

item.title: Don't expect a post-budget $1,250 boost this tax time
item.description: There was a lot of talk about the $1,000 instant tax reduction and the $250 Working Australians Tax Offset in the federal budget. But they won't be in play at tax time this year.

item.title: 'Missed' opportunities to support mental health of man fatally shot by police, inquest hears
item.description: The inquest heard Mr Donahue's mother had shared concerns about his mental health in the weeks leading up to his death.

item.title: Fluke, or are the Socceroos actually good?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Twins Jack and Artie Porter hear their mum for the first time
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Veteran prop James Slipper comes out of retirement for Wallabies at Nations Championship Tests
item.description: James Slipper comes out of retirement and three uncapped players are named for the Wallabies ahead of the Nations Championship Tests.

item.title: Why fuel excise cut will continue to lesser extent until August
item.description: The fuel excise cut was due to stop at the end of the month but will continue to a lesser extent until August. Here's why.

item.title: Cocaine bricks wash up on remote Fiji island
item.description: Twenty-seven bricks of cocaine have washed up around Fiji's remote Komo Island in recent weeks. Residents are worried about what the find will bring.

item.title: Planning on writing off an impulse EOFY sales buy? You won't get all your cash back at tax time
item.description: Advertisers tout EOFY sales as a prime time for big buys, and hint at the promise of a generous tax refund, but that's not quite how it works.

item.title: Agriculture Minister confirms another case of H5 bird flu in Western Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rugby Australia's new director of women's high-performance Tim Walsh sets eyes on 2029 World Cup
item.description: Only three years out from hosting the Women's Rugby World Cup, Australia makes a key appointment it hopes will help deliver success on the international stage.

item.title: First H5N1 bird flu case detected in Australia
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Passengers of hantavirus-hit cruise ship leave quarantine facility in WA
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Port Moresby's water supply network showing 'signs of imminent failure'
item.description: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

item.title: Vanuatu demands France hand back remote Matthew and Hunter Islands in the Pacific
item.description: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

item.title: A bassy Viking row and a living statue: The best World Cup fan traditions
item.description: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

item.title: Dutch fans take over US town amid World Cup frenzy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bosnian fans lined up outside World Cup match
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rich and famous families teach their kids Mandarin as China's influence grows
item.description: Rich and powerful families have a strong interest in one particular language, with experts saying it is no surprise given the country's rise.

item.title: A brain disease is striking down Aussie Rules players. At 23, Nick is the youngest
item.description: A talent and a team player, Nick Lowden excelled at Aussie Rules. But the knocks, bumps, hits and collisions were adding up.

item.title: How the Singha beer family is being torn apart by assault allegations
item.description: The billionaire family behind the Singha beer company is being torn apart by explosive allegations of assault and a century-old law that protects parents from neglectful children.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she has growing support in Canberra. But does it pass the pub test?
item.description: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

item.title: Curtin University program helps connect young autistic people to cyber security industry
item.description: The cybersecurity sector is projected to need tens of thousands of roles filled by 2030. Now, a university-based program is helping provide those workers by tapping into the abilities of autistic people.

item.title: Wallabies and All Blacks to play Bledisloe Cup Tests on Anzac Day at Lang Park
item.description: For the first time in 123 years, the Wallabies and All Blacks will play a Bledisloe Cup Test on Anzac Day, with Lang Park hosting in 2027, 2029 and 2031.

item.title: Thai teenager's family grieves as Australian charged with murder says death was 'out of my control'
item.description: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

item.title: Bali's beauty sinks beneath an 'Island of Trash' as waste issues increase
item.description: Known to many as the Island of the Gods, residents fear Bali may soon become the "Island of Trash" due to a deluge of tourists, a lack of rubbish education and the partial closure of a much-needed landfill facility.

item.title: Face paint, chants and flags — the many ways football fans celebrate the World Cup
item.description: From donning face paint to viral chants, each fandom has its peculiarities.

item.title: As Taylor struggles to handle Hanson, Hastie talks tough on One Nation
item.description: While Angus Taylor grapples with the Coalition's dilemma of how to keep the centre while diverting voters away from Pauline Hanson's party, Andrew Hastie has been talking tough on One Nation.

item.title: Coal and gas giants spending tens of millions on programs in schools and children's sport
item.description: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

item.title: Vanuatu critical infrastructure to remain 'free from militarisation' under Nakamal pact
item.description: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

item.title: Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carry Australia to T20 World Cup win over India
item.description: A powerful partnership by Ellyse Perry and Ash Gardner carries Australia to the greatest run chase in Women's T20 World Cup history, knocking India out of the tournament.

item.title: Ben Stokes retirement highlights his main character energy was not always a force for good
item.description: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

item.title: Crows star Chelsea Randall says concussion left with her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW
item.description: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

item.title: Superannuation switching trend sees billions flow out of retail funds, at a risk
item.description: There is now more than $1 trillion in self-managed superannuation funds, which means a big chunk of money isn't regulated by APRA, and it's the rate of the shift into SMSFs and less-regulated managed investment schemes that has regulators worried.

item.title: Blues coach Laurie Daley to answer critics after State of Origin III against Maroons
item.description: Laurie Daley says he will address criticism about his performance as Blues coach once the State of Origin series is over.

item.title: Billy Slater reveals stance on Maroons coaching future ahead of State of Origin III
item.description: Billy Slater says he will not sign another contract to be Queensland State of Origin coach, but that does not mean he will not continue in the role.

item.title: Lodging too early and other mistakes people make when doing their taxes
item.description: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

item.title: Ariane Beeston critiques ballet's toxic culture in new YA novel, Don't Forget to Smile
item.description: Ariane Beeston, the author of an acclaimed memoir detailing her experience with postpartum psychosis, has penned her first work of fiction, Don't Forget to Smile.

item.title: CTE poses a massive dilemma for the AFL. Experts say urgent action is needed to protect players
item.description: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

item.title: Are the wheels falling off the AI investment boom?
item.description: Overshadowing the tumult that is global politics has been a financial boom beyond almost anything witnessed before, focused entirely on the perceived benefits wrought by a brave new world of AI that threatens to up-end our lives.

item.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia from UK
item.description: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

item.title: Seven Australians in Venezuela request help as earthquake death toll rises
item.description: Australia is providing "consular assistance" to seven people after Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong confirms.

item.title: Retiring Ben Stokes backs Harry Brook as England's next Test captain
item.description: Ben Stokes says vice-captain Harry Brook has his "100 per cent" support to lead England's Test side, but coach Brendon McCullum is less effusive.

item.title: Ernst and Young staff sacked after Anthony Albanese's private banking information allegedly breached
item.description: Two Ernst and Young employees have been sacked after Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's personal banking information was allegedly breached while the pair worked for the accounting firm at the Commonwealth Bank.

item.title: World Cup: Lifeless Germany fall as Paraguay show tenacity to die another day
item.description: Paraguay's upset penalty-shootout win over Germany at the World Cup, while stunning, was not entirely unexpected, as they showed impressive determination to die another day.

item.title: Don't ask me what's culturally appropriate, I don't know either
item.description: After sending out invitations to a belated elopement party, I was suddenly fielding calls from friends who wanted to wear saris but were worried that it might be disrespectful.

item.title: Watchdog extends prohibition on Emily Lal providing freebirth services
item.description: Emily Lal, who previously operated online as The Authentic Birthkeeper, will be banned from advertising, providing advice and health services for another three months while the Victorian health watchdog investigates.

item.title: Fossil kept in drawer for 40 years is Antarctica’s first dinosaur bone
item.description: A bone found in Antarctica in 1985, and kept in a drawer in England for almost four decades, turns out to belong to one of the largest types of dinosaurs to ever walk the Earth.

item.title: Ahead of his Origin recall, can Bradman Best be New South Wales's saviour again?
item.description: In just two matches Bradman Best has earned a reputation as a State of Origin saviour and New South Wales will be desperate for a repeat of those heroics in the decider.

item.title: Sunraysia Football Netball League adopts new concussion app to improve transparency
item.description: A sporting league in regional Victoria is the first to adopt a concussion app aimed at improving communication and accountability around concussions at grassroots sports.

item.title: Melbourne childcare provider stripped of federal funding over serious safety breaches
item.description: The Melbourne childcare provider has been cut off under new powers that allow the federal government to withdraw funding due to safety breaches.

item.title: Scientists baffled at 60 per cent drop in migrating whale sightings off South West of WA
item.description: Marine scientists have expressed concerns following a 60 per cent drop in migrating whales seen off South West WA's coastline compared to previous years.

item.title: NT residents are the nation's happiest, university study finds
item.description: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

item.title: June sees biggest monthly fall in national housing values since 2022, Cotality finds
item.description: Australian home values record their biggest decline in more than three years as the housing market slowdown that began in Sydney and Melbourne spreads across the country.

item.title: US Supreme Court rejects Donald Trump's bid to restrict birthright citizenship
item.description: Donald Trump's controversial attempt to restrict birthright citizenship in the US is rejected by the Supreme Court, up-ending the president's hardline agenda on immigration.

item.title: How to protect your child from head knocks in sport
item.description: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

item.title: July 1 brings new rules, a tax cut and an increase to paid parental leave
item.description: A bunch of new rules come into effect today. Find out how they could impact your finances.

item.title: Photos reveal room where Australian man allegedly murdered Thai teenager
item.description: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman.

item.title: Coalition focuses fire on Labor, not One Nation, in daily talking points
item.description: Coalition talking points, obtained by the ABC, make almost no direct mention of One Nation despite the minor party surging in the polls.

item.title: From talking sheep to incel horror, here are the best movies of 2026 so far
item.description: It's officially halfway through 2026 and the year so far has brought cinematic delights as well as a fair share of silver-screen stinkers. Here are our critics' top picks.

item.title: Conversion practice ban legislation set to be debated in Tasmanian parliament
item.description: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

item.title: Australia hammers West Indies to claim a spot in the Women's T20 World Cup cricket final
item.description: Australia makes it through to another Women's T20 World Cup final, after a scary moment when West Indies star Deandra Dottin collapsed during the national anthems.

item.title: Venezuelan earthquake rescuers save three-year-old boy after six days underneath rubble
item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating two earthquakes has risen to more than 1,900 as tens of thousands of people still remain unaccounted for.

item.title: Maya Joint says walking with Serena Williams onto Wimbledon's centre court her 'craziest moment'
item.description: Australian Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams in the days leading up to their Wimbledon opener just to remove some of the mythos around the 23-time grand slam champion.

item.title: World Cup: French unity on display against Sweden as their ruthless run to title rolls on
item.description: In a French team littered with superstars it is something intangible that could stop them taking another World Cup title, following another clinical display against Sweden in the round of 32.

item.title: Vanuatu deal a sign of what's to come in 'permanent contest' for Pacific influence with China
item.description: Although a big moment in diplomacy, the Vanuatu agreement has come at a clear reputational cost to Australia.

item.title: World Cup: 'No tomorrow' as Socceroos brace for date with destiny
item.description: The Socceroos' World Cup adventure will either end or become historic when they face Egypt on Friday.

item.title: Is anyone happy with Australia's housing market? A new film asks the question
item.description: If you've been wondering how and why Australian house prices spun out of control you're not alone. One filmmaker was so depressed by it he went out and shot a documentary.

item.title: Chinese ambassador and ASIO trade barbs over foreign interference allegations
item.description: China's ambassador to Australia attacks ASIO over allegations of foreign interference aired in a video played at an agency event.

item.title: How Thailand's Pattaya City tried to shed its sex tourism hub reputation
item.description: It has become the centre of international attention after an Australian allegedly murdered a teenage girl, but Pattaya City has had an international reputation for decades.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Mexico madness at the Azteca, big Mo Salah injury update
item.description: Mexico fans enjoy a night to remember at the Azteca, Egypt gives a big update on Mo Salah's fitness, and Ronald Koeman quits as the Netherlands coach after a shootout disaster. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Women on being in a sexless marriage and tips for reconnecting
item.description: Women share how a lack of sex is impacting their marriage, and ways they're trying to spice things up.

item.title: Coalition labels review of 2025 election a 'hyper-partisan witch-hunt'
item.description: A Coalition group has launched an extraordinary attack on one of parliament's top committees, slamming its election inquiry as biased.

item.title: Prominent tech entrepreneur and former minister sentenced to jail in Indonesian corruption case
item.description: Indonesian tech entrepreneur and former education minister Nadiem Makarim has been sentenced to 10 years' jail in a corruption case linked to the procurement of Google Chromebooks.

item.title: ARN Media set to dump Karl Stefanovic from radio show, presenter defends controversial interview
item.description: Karl Stefanovic will be dumped from his new radio show amid fallout from his interview with Tommy Robinson, the ABC understands.

item.title: Village People founding member, lead singer Victor Willis dead at 74
item.description: The lead singer and a founding member of the Village People disco group has died at the age of 74.

item.title: Wednesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Police who handcuffed UK student Henry Nowak before he died being investigated for gross misconduct
item.description: Student Henry Nowak, 18, died while handcuffed by police in the English city of Southampton, after calling out "I can't breathe" and "I've been stabbed".

item.title: ‘Progressive patriotism’ central to Pat Conroy's AUKUS pitch to Labor faithful
item.description: Defence Industry Minister Pat Conroy is set to appeal to the Labor faithful to get behind AUKUS as he announces a defence shake-up targeting wasteful spending.

item.title: Recent years tougher for borrowers than enduring 17pc interest rates, analysis finds
item.description: Who had it tougher on housing? These stats may put the generational debate to bed.

item.title: USA's World Cup dream alive despite red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description: The US seals progression to the last 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina despite losing a man to a red card, while Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2.

item.title: Cheat Sheet: How Jackass delighted teens and terrified parents
item.description: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

item.title: Matthew Flinders's steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who circumnavigated Australia
item.description: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

item.title: Why this Vanuatu village worships a shape-shifting spirit called John Frum
item.description: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

item.title: Albanese and Chalmers see reform and risk-taking as an antidote to populism
item.description: The slight recovery in Labor's polling shifts assumptions about what's in store for the remainder of this parliamentary term.

item.title: Heading the ball is integral to football but concerns about its risks are growing
item.description: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

item.title: Up to 200 XPeng electric-vehicle owners still chasing $5,000 cashback promised months ago
item.description: When Mustansir Milky took the leap and bought a new electric vehicle, he didn't expect to have to fight to get the $5,000 cashback payment he was promised.

item.title: Tunchanok Donhomla's family lays Thai teen's ashes to rest and demands death penalty for alleged murder
item.description: The family of a Thai teenager allegedly killed by an Australian man has placed her cremated ashes to rest at a local Buddhist temple as they seek justice for her murder.

item.title: Satellite images reveal extent of Venezuela's earthquake devastation
item.description: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

item.title: Influencers to be targeted in Albanese government's gambling advertising crackdown
item.description: The Albanese government will target social media influencers and individuals who promote gambling on social media as part of its proposed laws restricting advertising.

item.title: Redress scheme will not clear backlog before closure at current processing rate
item.description: At its current processing rate, the scheme will not be able to clear its backlog before it closes.

item.title: Pornhub blocks advertising from Australian AI porn site OurDream
item.description: Australian pornography website OurDream has had its advertising suspended by Pornhub after the ABC revealed OurDream's content could include child exploitation material.

item.title: Government looks at encouraging turning empty shops into regional housing
item.description: As businesses shut up shop in regional main streets, the government is weighing up whether they could help solve the housing crisis.

item.title: World Cup hero Harry Kane leads England to final 16 with 2-1 victory over DR Congo
item.description: Facing World Cup elimination at the hands of DR Congo, England needed a hero to step up when it mattered most. Harry Kane was that man.

item.title: Russian daredevils climb Empire State Building to unfurl 'love' banner above New York
item.description: Two Russian daredevils, Angela Nikolau and Vanya Beerkus, claim to be the duo who scaled the 102-storey, 443-metre tower for an apparent show of love and a marriage proposal.

item.title: Liberals say minister should apologise for calling Menzies a 'Nazi appeaser' — as it happened
item.description: Liberal frontbencher James Paterson says Pat Conroy should apologise for comments made about former prime minister Robert Menzies. Take a look back at the day's coverage here.

item.title: Spider venom and gene disruptors to target varroa mite destroying Australia's honey bee colonies
item.description: Queensland researchers are testing novel ways to kill varroa mites, which have developed a resistance to standard treatments.

item.title: Wimbledon matches briefly interrupted as England fans watch World Cup on phones
item.description: England fans watch the football on their phones during Wimbledon, interrupting play briefly to cheer on Harry Kane.

item.title: ASX edges up while shoe retailer Betts shuts stores — as it happened
item.description: The ASX ends up following a sea of red on Wall Street.

item.title: US Navy helicopter goes down in Arabian Sea sparking search for missing crew member
item.description: A US Navy service member is missing and three others are injured after their MH-60S Sea Hawk helicopter went down in the Arabian Sea.

item.title: NT celebrates 2026 cracker night with a bang as fireworks light up the skies
item.description: People across the Northern Territory have spent the night setting off their own fireworks for the Territory's annual cracker night celebrations, lighting up the sky with colour and keeping firefighters and paramedics busy.

item.title: Novak Djokovic beats Stefanos Tsitsipas at Wimbledon, pranking ball kid along the way
item.description: A relaxed Novak Djokovic takes time out between sets of his second-round Wimbledon win over Stefanos Tsitsipas to play a prank on a ball kid.

item.title: Namadgi National Park shows signs of climate change as rangers intervene
item.description: Like melting glaciers in Antarctica or bleached coral on the Great Barrier Reef, Namadgi National Park is showing signs of damage linked to global warming.

item.title: Nick Kyrgios bows out of likely last Wimbledon in doubles loss with Alexander Bublik
item.description: Australian Nick Kyrgios says he has almost certainly played his final Wimbledon match after losing in the first round of the men's doubles alongside Alexander Bublik.

item.title: When do the Socceroos play next? How to watch Australia vs Egypt at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Lethal Weapon actor Danny Glover says he has Alzheimer's
item.description: Danny Glover, who turns 80 in July, has revealed that he has Alzheimer's disease and is leaning on his family for support as he lives with the incurable condition.

item.title: Car catches fire and fireworks explode out of boot
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ben Te Kura leaves Brisbane Broncos for NFL dream as Corey Jensen faces uncertain future
item.description: The Brisbane Broncos are releasing forward Ben Te Kura from the rest of his NRL contract to allow him to pursue his dream of playing in the NFL.

item.title: Financial misconduct compensation scheme faces $170 million cost blowout
item.description: The First Guardian and Shield super disasters, and Dixon Advisory fallout, is causing a cost blowout in the taxpayer-funded scheme that is designed to compensate victims of financial misconduct.

item.title: Christian Brothers granted pause on child abuse victim payouts due to financial strain
item.description: The Christian Brothers religious order has been granted a moratorium on all civil claims against it, after arguing it was running out of money to pay victims of child sexual abuse.

item.title: Mile Jedinak knows what it's like to take a World Cup penalty and he has advice for the Socceroos
item.description: Now an assistant coach for the Socceroos, and with a penalty shootout a possibility when Australia face Egypt in the round of 32, Mile Jedinak knows what it takes to seize the moment.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Feuding Belgians save the day, England's claims of 'disadvantage'
item.description: Belgium stars turn on each other before combining to save their team, England makes claims of "disadvantage", and DR Congo's goalkeeper gives it all he's got. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Firmus CEOs talk about energy use for planned AI factories
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Micro 'cockroach' drones help search for Venezuela earthquake survivors
item.description: A week after devastating twin earthquakes struck Venezuela rescue teams are using drones, dogs, cameras and specialised listening equipment to search for survivors.

item.title: Wallabies fullback Jock Campbell earns Test recall to face Ireland in Nations Championship opener
item.description: Jock Campbell emerges from almost four years in the international rugby wilderness to snare the prized fullback role for the Wallabies in their Nations Championship opener against Ireland.

item.title: $160 million government loan aims to protect an outback fertiliser industry
item.description: Ryowa Group has received a loan for its outback Queensland Phosphate Hill asset as the federal and state governments move to ramp up domestic fertiliser production capability.

item.title: Are parents reconsidering letting kids play footy due to CTE risks?
item.description: How is the conversation about lasting brain disease from repeated head knocks on the sporting field impacting parents' decisions about what sports their children play?

item.title: Government urged to crack down on NDIS kickbacks and bad actors as fraud inquiry handed down
item.description: A Labor-led parliamentary inquiry into fraud in the NDIS has made 12 recommendations to improve the integrity of the scheme.

item.title: Australia ranks third-highest for median wealth globally, UBS data shows
item.description: Australia holds the third-highest median wealth globally at $306,074, pipped at the post by Luxembourg and Belgium.

item.title: Former St Mary's Hostel in Alice Springs heritage listed as significant Stolen Generations site
item.description: Former residents say the heritage listing of the St Mary's Hostel site in Alice Springs is an important step towards healing, truth-telling and remembering the children taken there under assimilation-era policies.

item.title: NSW MP Helen Dalton's agricultural company fined over quad bike death
item.description: The court heard the 82-year-old worker was not wearing a helmet and the quad had no rollover protection when the crash occurred.

item.title: Oscar Piastri and Max Verstappen at centre of F1 driver change speculation
item.description: F1's rumour mill has been in overdrive in recent times about Oscar Piastri's future at McLaren, with his boss having to hose down suggestions the Australian will be driving anything other than a Papaya-coloured F1 car in 2027.

item.title: Russia strikes in central Kyiv kill at least 21 and injure dozens
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy cut his Dublin trip short as the strikes trapped paramedics and drivers at an ambulance station.

item.title: Catholic Schools NSW CEO Dallas McInerney to stand aside amid ICAC probe
item.description: The head of the body governing the state's catholic schools will stand aside after he was named in a corruption probe investigating donations to the NSW Liberal Party.

item.title: Dean Solomon yet to make decision on Bombers AFL coach role after James Hird confirms candidacy
item.description: James Hird's open ambitions have done little to sway Dean Solomon, with the interim Essendon coach yet to declare his future plans.

item.title: Look inside US President Donald Trump's new $580m Air Force One jet
item.description: The jet, gifted to the US government by Qatar, has been retrofitted, with Trump shaking off concerns over the cost.

item.title: Adelaide Thunderbirds fans will be in full cry at Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: Among the devoted Thunderbirds fans making the journey to Melbourne for the grand final against the Vixens are the Rawlins family, including sisters Tess and Eve.

item.title: Ex-Flinders Island school principal Gary Raymond Sykes jailed for child sex abuse
item.description: A retired Tasmanian school principal has been sentenced to four years in prison for child sex abuse.

item.title: Venezuelans make desperate pleas for help a week after devastating double earthquake
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man bids $16k to take his budget tax grudge to the tennis court with Albanese
item.description: A mortgage broker forked out $16,500 on Canberra’s night of nights to take his grudge about budget tax directly up to the prime minister in a tennis match at the Lodge.

item.title: Former Sydney childcare worker David James jailed for up to 12 years over child abuse offences
item.description: A painstaking victim identification process was undertaken by Australian Federal Police to establish his offending occurred at six of out-of-school hours care centres over a three-year period.

item.title: Prices rise at Alice Springs cattle sale, with steers fetching more than $6/kg
item.description: The strong results at the Alice Springs sale reflect the overall Australian cattle market, which has surged in recent weeks.

item.title: School worker indecently assaulted girls in Adelaide Hills, court told
item.description: A contract worker accused of indecently assaulted two students at an Adelaide Hills school he worked at has appeared in court for the first time.

item.title: eSafety commissioner tells royal commission Jewish community let down by cyber abuse scheme
item.description: eSafety Commissioner Julie Inman Grant became emotional while apologising to Jewish victims of social media harassment who do not meet the regulator's legal threshold for intervention.

item.title: Visa fees increase without warning, sparking outrage and disappointment
item.description: International students and families say they feel shocked and exploited after visa fees across dozens of categories rose on July 1 without warning, with some surging more than 200 per cent.

item.title: Lactalis fined by ACCC for misleading consumers about freshness of milk
item.description: One of Australia's largest milk providers has been fined by the consumer watchdog for misleading consumers with "fresh milk" labels on products that contained substantial amounts of powdered milk.

item.title: Senate orders Privacy Commissioner to release report on American Express security failures
item.description: The Privacy Commissioner has been ordered by parliament to hand over a secret report detailing its investigation into American Express's technology security failures, after using gag orders and legal threats to keep it confidential.

item.title: Teen accused of abducting 17yo girl near Melbourne school faces court
item.description: A teen has been accused of abducting a 17-year-old girl from near a Melbourne school with the intention of forcing her to marry him.

item.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids to pay large legal costs after royalties trial
item.description: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to be recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Lions hold on for win over Geelong in Kardinia classic
item.description: Brisbane Lions earn a second straight win over Geelong at Kardinia Park in a high-scoring epic, as the Lions jump into the top four.

item.title: John Dewar accepts UOW appointment would benefit KordaMentha, ICAC hears
item.description: ICAC hears that John Dewar wanted his UOW appointment to benefit KordaMentha and his acknowledgement there could be a perception of a conflict of interest.

item.title: Eight Thai monks killed after boy drives truck into procession in Mukdahan province
item.description: An 11-year-old has driven his parents' truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing eight monks.

item.title: New South Wales government cracks down on sales of potentially deadly nitrous oxide
item.description: After a 7.30 investigation exposed the increasing dangers of nitrous oxide use, NSW has announced reforms will come into place shortly.

item.title: Liberal MP Simon Kennedy says anti-gambling changes contain streaming 'loophole'
item.description: Liberal MP Simon Kennedy argues the federal government's proposed changes to gambling advertising laws contain a streaming loophole.

item.title: Vatican excommunicates members of breakaway group SSPX for ordaining bishops without papal approval
item.description: The Vatican has excommunicated priests and lay Catholics who are part of a breakaway right-wing Catholic group that ordained bishops without Pope Leo's approval.

item.title: De Minaur cruises through to Wimbledon third round with straight-sets win
item.description: Australian Alex de Minaur has raced into the third round at Wimbledon with a straight-sets victory over tricky French veteran Adrian Mannarino.

item.title: UK PM formally apologises over forced adoptions, calling the practice a 'stain on our history'
item.description: UK PM Keir Starmer has formally apologised for the British state's role in separating tens of thousands of unmarried mothers from their babies.

item.title: Security guard rescued from basement eight days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: A 43 year old man has been pulled from the rubble of a collapsed shopping centre after being trapped for eight days after earthquakes in Venezuela.

item.title: The best seasonal produce to look for in July
item.description: Here are some of the best fruit and vegetables to look for as we head further into winter.

item.title: Maggie O'Farrell sets her latest novel, Land, against the backdrop of the Great Famine in Ireland
item.description: The Academy Award-nominated writer dives into Irish history in Land, revealing how the Great Famine changed the country forever.

item.title: Live: Lamine Yamal's Spain faces Austria in World Cup knockout
item.description: Spain takes on Austria on Los Angeles for a place in the World Cup round of 16. Later on, Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal faces Croatia and Switzerland does battle with Algeria. Follow live.

item.title: Inside Venezuela anger and desperation are rising. The number of corpses is 'incalculable'
item.description: On the ground in Venezuela death and desperation linger but they are juxtaposed against miracle survival stories.

item.title: What a fall in house prices would mean for Australians
item.description: After decades of growth Australian house prices are slowing. Here's what a fall could look like.
